source.title: Reviewed educational chest pain case
source.citation: Local reviewed educational case file for Diagnostician demo content.
source.raw_text: De-identified educational case source assembled and reviewed for demo validation.

case.title: Pressure-like chest pain with diaphoresis
case.chief_complaint: Acute pressure-like chest pain with nausea and sweating.
teaching_points: Pressure-like chest pain with diaphoresis should prompt immediate ECG review.
Inferior ST elevation with reciprocal changes is a time-sensitive reperfusion pattern.
Management should connect antiplatelet therapy, anticoagulation, and catheterization activation to the source ECG.
curation_notes: Manually authored and reviewed demo case with ECG, treatment, consult, and observation paths.

facts.label: Opening concern
facts.value: The patient reports heavy central chest pressure with nausea and sweating.

provenance.quote: Acute pressure-like chest pain with nausea and sweating.

facts.label: Symptom onset
facts.value: The pain began suddenly 45 minutes before arrival while he was walking upstairs.

provenance.quote: Pain began 45 minutes before arrival while climbing stairs.

facts.label: Associated symptoms
facts.value: He feels short of breath and lightheaded but denies pleuritic pain or tearing back pain.

provenance.quote: Shortness of breath and lightheadedness; no pleuritic or tearing back pain.

facts.label: Cardiac risk history
facts.value: History includes hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and active tobacco use.

provenance.quote: Hypertension, hyperlipidemia, active smoker.

facts.label: Home medications
facts.value: He takes lisinopril and atorvastatin but missed several recent statin doses.

provenance.quote: Home medications: lisinopril and atorvastatin, with missed statin doses.

facts.label: Triage vitals
facts.value: Blood pressure is 92/58 mmHg, heart rate 48/min, respiratory rate 20/min, oxygen saturation 95% on room air, temperature 36.8 C.

facts.label: Cardiopulmonary exam
facts.value: He is pale and diaphoretic. Lungs are clear and there is no asymmetric pulse deficit.

provenance.quote: Pale, diaphoretic, clear lungs, no asymmetric pulse deficit.

facts.label: Initial ECG
facts.value: ECG shows 2 mm ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF with reciprocal ST depression in I and aVL.

provenance.quote: 2 mm ST elevation in II, III, aVF with reciprocal ST depression in I and aVL.

facts.label: Right-sided ECG
facts.value: Right-sided leads show 1 mm ST elevation in V4R, suggesting right ventricular involvement.

provenance.quote: Right-sided V4R has 1 mm ST elevation.

facts.label: Initial troponin
facts.value: Initial high-sensitivity troponin is mildly elevated at 68 ng/L.

provenance.quote: Initial hs-troponin 68 ng/L.

facts.label: Basic labs
facts.value: Creatinine is 1.0 mg/dL, potassium 4.2 mmol/L, and hemoglobin 14.6 g/dL.

facts.label: Chest radiograph
facts.value: Portable chest radiograph shows no widened mediastinum, edema, or focal consolidation.

provenance.quote: No widened mediastinum, pulmonary edema, or focal consolidation.

facts.label: Antiplatelet therapy
facts.value: He receives aspirin and a P2Y12 loading dose while catheterization is activated.

provenance.quote: Aspirin and P2Y12 load given with cath lab activation.

facts.label: Fluids and nitrates
facts.value: A small IV fluid bolus is given and nitrates are avoided because of hypotension and right-sided ECG changes.

provenance.quote: Small IV fluid bolus; nitrates avoided with hypotension and right-sided changes.

facts.label: Cardiology activation
facts.value: Interventional cardiology accepts the patient for immediate catheterization.

provenance.quote: Interventional cardiology accepted for immediate catheterization.

facts.label: Repeat assessment
facts.value: After fluids, blood pressure improves to 104/64 mmHg but chest pressure persists.

provenance.quote: BP improved to 104/64 after fluids; chest pressure persisted.

facts.label: Catheterization finding
facts.value: Coronary angiography identifies an acute thrombotic occlusion of the proximal right coronary artery.

provenance.quote: Acute thrombotic occlusion of the proximal RCA.

facts.label: Dangerous differential
facts.value: A time-sensitive coronary syndrome should remain high on the differential with this pressure-like pain and diaphoresis pattern.

provenance.quote: Pressure-like chest pain and diaphoresis support dangerous coronary syndrome consideration.

facts.label: Hint 1
facts.value: Prioritize the first test that can localize time-sensitive ischemia at the bedside.

provenance.quote: Bedside ECG is key in pressure-like chest pain with diaphoresis.

facts.label: Hint 2
facts.value: The combination of hypotension, bradycardia, and inferior-lead changes should affect medication choices.

provenance.quote: Hypotension, bradycardia, inferior changes alter treatment choices.

facts.label: Final diagnosis
facts.value: Inferior ST-elevation myocardial infarction with right ventricular involvement.

provenance.quote: Final diagnosis: inferior ST-elevation myocardial infarction with right ventricular involvement.
